mnemonics: Remember KARAKA as 'Key Actors Roles And Kinships Among words' to recall it's about semantic relationships between nouns and verbs in a sentence.
keyPoints: Karaka theory defines six semantic relationships (karakas) between verbs and nouns in a sentence.
It is a core concept from the ancient Indian linguistic text, Ashtadhyayi, by Panini.
The theory provides a framework for analyzing sentence structure that is different from Western grammatical cases.
commonMistakes: Confusing karakas with the standard grammatical cases (nominative, accusative, etc.) used in English or Latin.
Assuming the number of karakas is the same as the number of cases in other grammatical systems.
Overlooking that karaka theory is based on semantic role (meaning) rather than just syntactic position.
